prompt: A listener asked whether using a low incline instead of flat bench might improve hypertrophy, especially considering arching/retraction and shoulder comfort.
assertions: Notes that safety claims about one setup being clearly safer are not strongly established
Identifies longer range of motion as a plausible mechanism for low incline advantage
Mentions that low incline changes the touch point upward, which can increase pec/triceps excursion
Provides practical takeaway: use pressing angle(s) that allow training hard through good ROM with manageable discomfort
Acknowledges low incline may be modestly better but with limited certainty
